commit.message:
Fix keepalive: use Supabase JS client + daily schedule

The curl-based REST API ping was not registering as real activity
for Supabase's pause detection. Switching to:
1. Supabase JS client for proper DB connection
2. Both a read query AND auth healthcheck
3. Daily schedule instead of twice/week for safety

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: garmin-mcp-server
repository.description: MCP

commit.message:
Fix keepalive schedule: use Mon/Thu instead of */3 day-of-month

Day-of-month cron can leave gaps > 7 days. Switching to fixed
weekdays ensures consistent 3-4 day intervals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: lambda error handling improvement

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix flaky Google Drive sync test by enforcing RESYNC on WebRTC peer connect (#8098)

* Initial plan

* Fix flaky Google Drive sync test by waiting for WebRTC peer connections

The "should realtime sync on both sides" test randomly fails because
mutation operations start before WebRTC signaling peers are connected.
Without connected peers, notifyPeers() RESYNC signals are lost and
the remote collection relies on polling with increasing backoff delays,
which can exceed the 8000ms assertion timeout.

Add explicit waitUntil() calls to ensure both peers' WebRTC connections
are established before starting insert/delete operations, following the
same pattern used in the signaling unit test.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix signaling to enforce RESYNC on peer connect and guard against unconnected peers

Two issues in the signaling code caused the flaky test:

1. pingPeers() called peer.send() on not-yet-connected peers in
   peerBySenderId (peers are added to the map on creation, before
   WebRTC connection is established). This throws an error. Fixed
   by checking peer.connected before sending.

2. When a WebRTC peer connects, only the local side resynced. The
   remote peer was not told to RESYNC, so if data was pushed while
   the connection was being established, the notification was lost.
   Fixed by sending 'RESYNC' to the peer in the connect handler.

Both fixes applied to Google Drive and OneDrive signaling code.
Test file reverted to its original state.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Suppress warn_unused_result warnings with (void)! cast

Wrap standalone write(), read(), and posix_memalign() calls with
(void)! to explicitly mark their return values as intentionally
ignored. GCC's __attribute__((warn_unused_result)) is not suppressed
by a plain (void) cast, but applying ! first converts the result to
int, which the (void) cast then silences.

Files changed:
- src/test_functional.c
- src/test_consistency.c
- src/test_exception.c
- src/test_concurrent.c
- src/test_stress.c
- src/test_performance.c

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix performance issues in news and filings backfill

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in backfill_news_polygon,
  backfill_news_finnhub, and backfill_8k_for_ticker, replacing per-record
  execute() loops. News is the highest-volume dataset so this has the
  most impact.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_8k_filings to reduce pagination round-trips,
  mirroring the same fix applied to corporate actions endpoints.

- Eliminate redundant dict copy in _extract_sentiment: was doing
  enriched = dict(article) on every article before adding 2 keys.
  Since news.py re-extracts sentiment from insights itself, the copy was
  wasted work. Now mutates in-place instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix audit logs real-time updates and OMR scanner alignment error handling

- Replace one-time getDocs fetch with onSnapshot in AuditLogs.tsx for real-time updates
- Add subscribeLogs() method to AuditLogger service using Firestore onSnapshot
- Show alignment error on camera mode instead of results mode
- Return early when corner markers not detected to prevent misaligned bubble markers

commit.message:
Fix memory leak in corporate actions backfill

- Cache tenacity retry wrapper as self._retrying_execute in PolygonClient.__init__
  instead of recreating a new closure on every HTTP request. Hundreds of wrapper
  objects were accumulating during pagination loops, causing GC pressure and slowness.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_dividends, fetch_splits, fetch_short_interest to
  reduce pagination round-trips (Polygon default page size is small).

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in all three backfill_*_for_ticker functions
  instead of a per-record execute() loop.

- Wrap PolygonClient in a with context manager in run_full_backfill so the
  httpx.Client connection pool is always released on exit.

- Add regression tests: retry wrapper identity check across requests, limit param
  assertions on all three corporate actions fetch methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Mobile navbar: side-by-side buttons, outlined REGISTER, divider
